queue.task: HenryDB: test persistent DB through pg wire
queue.summary: Full stack end-to-end: pg driver → PG wire → SQL engine → WAL → persistent storage → close → reopen → data recovered. Complete database lifecycle from client to disk and back.

queue.summary: ORM-like CRUD via pg wire: schema creation, INSERT RETURNING, parameterized SELECT, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP defaults all work. Bug found: JOIN with column aliases crashes pg driver (wire protocol field descriptor issue). CRUD on single tables is fully functional.

queue.task: HenryDB: explore query plan visualization or advanced optimizer features
queue.summary: Explored EXPLAIN/EXPLAIN ANALYZE and optimizer. Findings: (1) EXPLAIN ANALYZE has rich output with per-operator timing, IO stats. (2) BUG: Classic plan vs Volcano plan disagree on scan type for non-selective queries. (3) AND predicates correctly pick better index. (4) OR predicates fall back to SeqScan (no bitmap OR). (5) Subquery IN uses default 33% estimate. (6) Histogram-based estimation working well for equality/range.

queue.task: Session C final wrap
HenryDB: investigate the EXPLAIN classic vs Volcano plan discrepancy bug
queue.summary: Investigated EXPLAIN discrepancy: Classic plan and Volcano plan are generated by independent code paths with different cost models. Classic uses selectivity threshold (<=0.5 -> index); Volcano uses proper cost-based comparison with page IO costs. Volcano is correct. Fix: unify by having classic display use Volcano decisions.

queue.task: HenryDB: stress test concurrent MVCC scenarios (write skew, phantom reads)
queue.summary: MVCC stress testing results: (1) Write skew occurs (expected for SI, needs SERIALIZABLE). (2) No phantom reads (snapshot isolation correct). (3) Write-write conflict prevents lost updates (first-writer-wins at UPDATE time). (4) Read-only snapshots stable across concurrent commits. (5) Deadlock prevented by write-write conflict (no blocking, fails fast). Summary: solid SI implementation, write skew is the main gap (needs SERIALIZABLE isolation level).

queue.task: HenryDB: explore window functions, CTEs, and recursive queries for edge cases
queue.summary: Window functions, CTEs, recursive queries all work well. Tested: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, SUM OVER, running totals, LAG, NTILE, simple/multi/recursive CTEs, Fibonacci, org tree traversal, CTE+Window combo, EXISTS, scalar subquery, CASE+Window, multi-window. One gap: recursive CTE cycles handled by depth limit only (no UNION-based cycle detection). All features solid.

queue.task: HenryDB: benchmark INSERT performance and identify bottlenecks
queue.summary: INSERT benchmark: 50/sec auto-commit (20ms each), 1600/sec in tx (0.6ms each). 32x faster in tx. Inner DB without MVCC/WAL: 0.28ms (66x faster). Bottleneck: fs.writeSync per WAL record. Each INSERT = 256 bytes WAL. Bug found: unrecognized syncMode values silently disable sync. Fix: write buffering or async WAL.

queue.task: neural-net: investigate MAML meta-learning instability - add gradient clipping
queue.summary: Added gradient clipping to MAML (L2 norm, default 10.0). Testing showed clipping helps reduce variance but main instability is from task sampling noise, not gradient magnitude. Batch size 16 reduces variance 14x but doesnt improve convergence. FOMAML on linear tasks converges quickly then plateaus. All 11 tests pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: test advanced SQL features - LATERAL, EXCEPT, INTERSECT, full outer join
queue.summary: All 22 advanced SQL features work: UNION/ALL, INTERSECT, EXCEPT, FULL OUTER JOIN, CROSS JOIN, LEFT/RIGHT JOIN, NATURAL JOIN, LATERAL, COALESCE, CASE, GROUP BY null, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE, IS NULL, self-join, multi-aggregate, correlated subquery, DISTINCT, ORDER BY expression. Zero failures. Very comprehensive SQL implementation.

queue.task: neural-net: explore NTM on actual sequence learning task (copy, sort)
queue.summary: NTM exploration: (1) Copy task: untrained NTM cant copy sequences (expected) but outputs differ per input (memory is used). (2) No training infrastructure (forward-only, no BPTT). (3) MemoryBank as associative memory: exact read/write, content-based retrieval works (8/8 capacity test), overwrite works. Memory mechanism is solid; NTM would need BPTT training to learn tasks.

queue.task: HenryDB: stress test WAL recovery with crash simulation
queue.summary: WAL recovery stress tests: (1) Post-checkpoint ops (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) recovered correctly. (2) Uncommitted tx properly rolled back on recovery. (3) Truncated WAL: graceful recovery, partial records skipped (9/10 rows recovered). (4) Multiple checkpoints: all data recovered. WAL system is robust.

queue.task: HenryDB: test compiled query engine vs volcano engine performance
queue.summary: Compiled vs Volcano benchmark: Compiled is 33-49x faster than TransactionalDB (MVCC overhead dominates). Fair comparison without MVCC: compiled is 1.3x faster than Volcano interpreter. Result cache makes repeated queries near-instant. Bottleneck is MVCC+WAL layer (20ms per query), not query execution itself.

queue.task: HenryDB: explore edge cases in parser - unicode, long strings, nested expressions
queue.summary: Parser edge case testing: Unicode+emoji work. Escaped quotes work. Deeply nested expressions work. NULL semantics correct. Division by zero returns NULL. BUG FOUND: strings >4068 bytes silently truncated/lost (no error). Max reliable string = 4068 bytes (4KB page limit). This is a data corruption risk - should throw error for oversized values.

queue.task: neural-net: profile training loop performance on different network sizes
queue.summary: Neural-net performance profiling: Forward scales linearly with params (49p=0.08ms, 263K=24ms). Train is 3-4x slower than forward. Batch scales linearly. Deep nets faster per-param than wide. XL (263K params): 80ms/step = 12 steps/sec. Reasonable for pure JS. Bottleneck is Matrix multiplication (no BLAS/GPU).

queue.task: HenryDB: test adaptive engine and prepared statements
queue.summary: Prepared statements work well: PREPARE/EXECUTE/DEALLOCATE. $1 params, dealloc prevents re-use. Adaptive engine not connected to TransactionalDB. Result cache works at inner DB level (0.004ms cache hit) but TransactionalDB MVCC overhead (~20ms) negates benefit. This confirms MVCC overhead is the dominant cost.

queue.task: HenryDB: explore trigger and view edge cases under MVCC
queue.summary: Triggers and views work correctly under MVCC. Trigger INSERT/UPDATE fire properly with OLD/NEW. Trigger effects rolled back with tx rollback. Views are virtual, reflect changes, work in transactions. All 6 scenarios passed.

queue.task: HenryDB: count all features and tests, write summary of the entire database
queue.summary: HenryDB stats: 369 source files (78,631 LOC), 872 test files (128,642 LOC), 8,157 test cases in 1,890 suites. Total: 207K LOC across 1,241 files. Features span: SQL parser, MVCC, WAL, B-tree/heap storage, indexes, triggers, views, CTEs, window functions, prepared statements, compiled query engine, Volcano executor, cost-based optimizer, EXPLAIN ANALYZE, FK cascades, and 50+ data structure implementations.

queue.task: neural-net: end-to-end training demo (XOR, MNIST digits, classification)
queue.summary: End-to-end training demos: XOR 100% accuracy (1K epochs). 3-class spiral 99% accuracy (300 epochs). Sin(x) regression MSE 0.13 - learns shape but not precise (needs LR scheduling or tanh activations). All demos work out of the box with the Network API. Training API: net.dense().loss().train({inputs, targets}).

queue.task: HenryDB: explore data structure implementations (B-tree, LSM, bloom filter)
queue.summary: All 6 data structures work correctly: B+Tree (range scan, 1.6M ins/s, 3.3M srch/s), SkipList (1M/2.5M), AVL (optimal height=6, 1.5M/5.1M fastest search), ART (prefix trie, 0.8M/1.6M), LSM (write-optimized, 0.7M/1.9M), BloomFilter (0% FP on 1K test). All within 10x of JS Map (7.4M/16.5M). Solid educational implementations.

queue.task: HenryDB: test JSON support, array operations, and type system
queue.summary: JSON/Type system: JSON_EXTRACT, ARRAY_LENGTH, JSON_TYPE, JSON_VALID work. JSON operators (->,->>,'#>') parse but return NULL. ARRAY not functional (literal returns string). Boolean/CAST/TYPEOF work. STRFTIME works, DATE(now) returns null. Missing: JSON_KEYS, JULIANDAY, PRINTF, UNICODE, ANY(), UNNEST. Core type system solid, JSON partially implemented.

queue.task: HenryDB: profile TransactionalDB overhead - identify which MVCC operations cost most
queue.summary: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: TransactionalDB routes SELECT through auto-commit DML path (begin+execute+WAL+commit+physicalize). Parse=0.009ms, MVCC=0.002ms, Inner DB=0.3ms — the 18.6ms overhead is WAL writes + physicalize for reads that don't need it. Fix: add read-only fast path for SELECT (snapshot read only, no WAL, no begin/commit). This would make SELECT 60x faster.
